name: Denmark-Identity-Fallback Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Non-production demonstrator prototype availability → Systemic: Reduced platform dependence in AltID design documents → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty and supplier diversification.
options: Establish a fallback authentication path using open standards (e.g., FIDO2/WebAuthn, OpenID Connect) on a privacy-respecting Android-compatible platform like GrapheneOS.
Develop a browser-based or hardware-token-based fallback authentication model to reduce dependence on the mobile OS duopoly.
Implement an emerging tech-based solution leveraging blockchain and decentralized identity management for a truly platform-neutral approach.
review: Controls dependency vs. independence. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact of incumbent resistance from existing operators.

name: Danish-Procurement-Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formal written response from Digitaliseringsstyrelsen → Systemic: Procurement language inclusion in AltID-related design documents → Strategic: Reduced supplier concentration and increased digital sovereignty.
options: Influence procurement processes to include platform-neutrality and fallback access requirements for critical public authentication.
Engage with relevant EU bodies and technical working groups connected to the European Digital Identity Wallet framework.
Advocate for the inclusion of open standards (e.g., FIDO2/WebAuthn, OpenID Connect) in AltID design documents.
review: Controls procurement vs. supplier concentration. Weakness: The options fail to address potential regulatory friction and incumbent resistance.

name: Digital-Identity-Standards Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Standards pressure on existing operators → Systemic: Alignment of Danish advocacy with broader European requirements → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty and reduced platform lock-in.
options: Engage with EU bodies to advocate for platform-neutral access or certified fallback mechanisms in the European Digital Identity Wallet framework.
Influence procurement processes to include open standards (e.g., FIDO2/WebAuthn, OpenID Connect) in AltID design documents.
Develop a set of technical guidelines and standards for platform-neutral authentication.
review: Controls standards vs. interoperability. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on existing infrastructure and operator interests.

name: Regulatory-Alignment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Formal written response from Digitaliseringsstyrelsen → Systemic: Alignment of Danish advocacy with broader European requirements → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty and reduced regulatory friction.
options: Engage with relevant EU bodies to align Danish advocacy with broader European requirements on interoperability, portability, resilience, and reduced platform lock-in.
Influence procurement processes to include platform-neutrality and fallback access requirements for critical public authentication.
Advocate for regulatory changes to support platform-neutral authentication.
review: Controls regulation vs. flexibility. Weakness: The options fail to address potential incumbent resistance and financing uncertainty.

name: Coalition-Building Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Coalition formation with civil-society organisations, privacy advocates, academic researchers → Systemic: Enhanced digital sovereignty through collective advocacy → Strategic: Reduced supplier concentration and increased resilience.
options: Build a coalition spanning civil-society organisations, privacy advocates, academic researchers, security experts, contingency-planning stakeholders, and sympathetic members of Folketinget's digital policy committee.
Engage with relevant EU bodies to advocate for platform-neutral access or certified fallback mechanisms in the European Digital Identity Wallet framework.
Influence procurement processes to include open standards (e.g., FIDO2/WebAuthn, OpenID Connect) in AltID design documents.
review: Controls coalition vs. influence. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on existing power dynamics and operator interests.

name: Digital-Sovereignty-Alignment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced platform dependence → Systemic: Increased resilience → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.
options: Align with EU Digital Identity Wallet framework
Embed platform-neutral requirements in procurement documents
Develop a national digital identity standards body
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. incumbent resistance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the impact on existing operators' business models.

name: Ecosystem-Partnership Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Stronger coalition building → Systemic: Enhanced industry collaboration → Strategic: Increased market influence.
options: Form a public-private partnership for digital identity development
Establish a joint research and development fund with industry partners
Create a shared standards body for digital identity
review: Controls public-private partnerships vs. regulatory capture. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for unequal resource distribution among partners.

name: Risk-Assessment Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Identified security risks → Systemic: Improved risk management → Strategic: Enhanced digital identity resilience.
options: Conduct a comprehensive risk assessment of the current digital identity infrastructure
Develop a risk-based approach to digital identity development and deployment
Establish a continuous monitoring program for digital identity systems
review: Controls security risks vs. operational costs. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for false negatives in risk assessments.

name: Regulatory-Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Clarified regulatory requirements → Systemic: Increased compliance → Strategic: Enhanced digital identity governance.
options: Develop a national digital identity regulatory framework
Establish clear guidelines for digital identity development and deployment
Create a shared understanding of digital identity risks and mitigation strategies
review: Controls regulatory clarity vs. bureaucratic complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for regulatory lag in addressing emerging technologies.

name: Standards-Development Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Established digital identity standards → Systemic: Increased interoperability → Strategic: Enhanced digital identity ecosystem.
options: Develop a national digital identity standards body
Establish clear guidelines for digital identity development and deployment
Create a shared understanding of digital identity risks and mitigation strategies
review: Controls standards development vs. industry resistance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for conflicting standards among stakeholders.

name: Procurement-Intervention Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Aligned procurement processes → Systemic: Increased supplier diversity → Strategic: Enhanced digital identity resilience.
options: Embed platform-neutral requirements in procurement documents
Develop a national digital identity procurement framework
Create a shared understanding of digital identity risks and mitigation strategies
review: Controls procurement processes vs. incumbent resistance. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for unequal resource distribution among suppliers.

name: Innovation-Incentivization Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Encouraged innovation → Systemic: Increased digital identity resilience → Strategic: Enhanced digital sovereignty.
options: Establish a funding program for digital identity innovation
Create a shared understanding of digital identity risks and mitigation strategies
Develop a national digital identity innovation hub
review: Controls innovation incentives vs. regulatory complexity. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential for unequal resource distribution among innovators.

name: Digital-Identity-Framework Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Reduced dependence on foreign tech suppliers → Systemic: Enhanced digital sovereignty → Strategic: Increased resilience and continuity of service.
options: Decouple from Apple/Google's trust model, adopting a standards-based approach.
Incorporate open-source identity management solutions.
Implement a hybrid model leveraging both proprietary and open-source components.
review: Controls supplier concentration vs. platform lock-in. Weakness: The options fail to consider the potential impact on existing operator business models.

name: Procurement-Intervention Strategy
consequences: Immediate: Modified procurement processes → Systemic: Supplier-neutral requirements → Strategic: Reduced platform lock-in.
options: Influence AltID to include platform-neutrality and fallback access requirements.

Develop a risk management framework for future digital identity projects.
Create a contingency plan for potential platform outages or changes.

Create a regulatory environment encouraging supplier diversity and competition.

Create an ecosystem of suppliers promoting platform neutrality and fallback access.
